new_value: Created 4 feature tasks decomposing the admin UI epic: Bantu-hjx (schema), Bantu-4kv (HTTP server), Bantu-fti (REST API), Bantu-3j8 (web frontend). Dependency chain established. Design documented in epic comment.

{"description":"Add AdminConfig Pydantic model to nanobot/config/schema.py and include it in GatewayConfig.

Fields:
- enabled: bool = False
- token: str = ''  # Bearer token; empty = no auth required
- host: str = '127.0.0.1'  # Bind to localhost by default for security
- port: int = 18791  # Separate port from gateway messaging port

Schema fix (REQUIRED as part of this task):
- Remove the duplicate MatrixConfig class currently defined twice in schema.py (lines 67 and 188). Keep the first definition (lines 67-83) — it is the complete one. Merge any unique fields from the second definition before deleting it.

Gateway startup behaviour:
- Print admin URL to console when admin.enabled=True.
- If admin.token is empty, print a prominent warning alongside the URL:
    [yellow]⚠ Admin UI running with NO authentication — any local process can read/write config[/yellow]
  This alerts operators before they expose the admin interface in a shared environment.

Write unit tests for schema serialization (snake_case + camelCase round-trips) covering AdminConfig and the merged MatrixConfig."}

new_value:
{"description":"Create nanobot/admin/ module with an AdminServer class backed by aiohttp.

Deliverables:
- nanobot/admin/__init__.py
- nanobot/admin/server.py — AdminServer(config: Config) with start()/stop() methods
  - aiohttp Application setup
  - Bearer token middleware: reads Authorization header, returns 401 if token mismatch (only when config.gateway.admin.token is set)
  - CORS policy (MUST be exact — do NOT use wildcard origins):
    - Allowed origins: http://127.0.0.1:18791 and http://localhost:18791 only
    - Access-Control-Allow-Origin: set to the request Origin only if it matches one of the two allowed origins; otherwise omit the header
    - Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS
    - Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type
    - Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: false (bearer token in Authorization header makes the credentials flag unnecessary)
    - Rationale: a wildcard CORS policy (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) would allow any website the user visits to silently read or overwrite config via cross-site requests
  - Serves static admin UI from nanobot/admin/static/index.html at GET /
- Add aiohttp>=3.9.0 to pyproject.toml dependencies
- Integrate AdminServer into the gateway CLI command (start after channels, stop on shutdown)
- Print admin URL to console if admin.enabled=True; if admin.token is empty also print a prominent warning (see Bantu-hjx)
- Unit tests for auth middleware (valid token, invalid token, no-token-configured bypass) and CORS header correctness"}
old_value:
{"id":"Bantu-4kv","title":"Feature: Admin HTTP server with aiohttp and bearer-token auth","description":"Create nanobot/admin/ module with an AdminServer class backed by aiohttp.

  - CORS headers for browser requests (localhost origins)
  - Serves static admin UI from nanobot/admin/static/index.html at GET /
- Add aiohttp>=3.9.0 to pyproject.toml dependencies
- Integrate AdminServer into the gateway CLI command (start after channels, stop on shutdown)
- Print admin URL to console if admin.enabled=True
- Unit tests for auth middleware (valid token, invalid token, no-token-configured bypass)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T14:47:34Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T14:47:34Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Implement REST API route handlers in nanobot/admin/routes.py for managing gateway configuration.

Endpoints:
- GET  /api/config                  Return sanitized config (all sensitive fields masked with '****')
- GET  /api/providers               List providers with {name, label, has_key, api_base, is_oauth, is_local}
- PUT  /api/providers/{name}        Update provider: {api_key?, api_base?, extra_headers?}; persists to config.json
- GET  /api/channels                List channels with {name, enabled, summary}
- GET  /api/channels/{name}         Full channel config dict (sensitive tokens masked)
- PUT  /api/channels/{name}         Merge-update channel config, persist to disk
- GET  /api/mcp                     List MCP servers with name and {command, url, tool_timeout}
- POST /api/mcp/{name}              Create new MCP server entry
- PUT  /api/mcp/{name}              Update existing MCP server
- DELETE /api/mcp/{name}            Remove MCP server entry
- GET  /api/agent                   Return agent defaults
- PUT  /api/agent                   Update agent defaults (model, temperature, max_tokens, etc.)

Implementation notes:
- Concurrency safety (REQUIRED): Declare a module-level lock in routes.py:
    _config_lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()
  Every write handler MUST acquire this lock before loading config and hold it until after save_config() returns:
    async with _config_lock:
        cfg = load_config()
        # ... mutate cfg ...
        save_config(cfg)
  This prevents concurrent PUT requests from racing and silently discarding each other's changes.
- All reads go through load_config(); all writes call save_config() after validation, wrapped in _config_lock
- Sensitive field masking: any key containing 'token', 'key', 'secret', 'password', 'credential' → '****'
- Validation: invalid JSON body → 400; unknown provider → 404; unknown channel → 404
- Unit tests for each endpoint group: providers CRUD, channels CRUD, mcp CRUD, agent settings; include a concurrency test that fires two simultaneous PUTs and verifies both changes are persisted"}
old_value:

new_value:
{"description":"Implement a single-page HTML/CSS/JS admin console served at GET / by the admin server.

File: nanobot/admin/static/index.html (bundled as package data)

UI Sections:
1. API Keys — list all LLM providers; show key presence (masked); inline edit field to update; save button per row
2. Chat Integrations — list all channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.); toggle enabled; expand to reveal and edit config fields; save per channel
3. MCP Servers — table of configured servers; Add button (dialog with name, command/args or URL fields); Edit and Delete per row
4. Agent Settings — form for model, provider, temperature, max_tokens, max_tool_iterations, memory_window, reasoning_effort; single Save button

Design requirements:
- Vanilla HTML5 + CSS + JavaScript (no build step, no frameworks)
- Bearer token prompt on first load if admin token is configured (stored in sessionStorage for convenience)
- Token lifecycle rules (MUST follow exactly):
  (a) Auth is always enforced server-side — the UI stores the token only for UX convenience, never as a security gate
  (b) Every fetch() call sends the stored token in the Authorization header; if the server returns 401, the UI MUST immediately re-prompt for the token (do not show an opaque error or hide the UI)
  (c) Never add a client-side gate that hides the UI or skips rendering based on whether a token is stored — all protection comes from the server's 401 responses
- fetch() API for all REST calls
- Inline save feedback (green checkmark / red error message)
- Responsive layout (works on desktop and mobile)
- Clean, minimal design matching nanobot's aesthetic (dark-friendly, use system fonts)

Testing:
- Manual smoke test (no automated unit test for pure HTML — integration covered by API tests)
- Update pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build] include to bundle nanobot/admin/static/** files"}
old_value:

{"id":"Bantu-msf","title":"Gateway server should allow empty Config and prompt user to add mandatory config","description":"Gateway command should not raise error if the config.json is empty Currently, it exits with an error 'Error: No API key configured'","acceptance_criteria":"`uv run gateway` should not exit with error if config.json is not configured","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T16:26:39Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T16:26:39Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Add support for specialized agents. Current default agent will be the personal assistant. 
- Ability to override provider config for specialized agent. Specific config overrides Default config
- Ability to set config via admin UI
- Agents are defined under /agents folder. Each folder represents one agent. All assets of agent are stored under it
- Session should be owned by only one agent
- Ability of inbound messages should be scoped to specific agent
- Specialized agents should be able to send to all clients
- Load/Store agent specific runtime configuration/memory under .bantu folder
- Specialized agents should never update default assests used by default agent
- Cron/Hearbeat service should always be run by default agent"}

{"id":"Bantu-g1q","title":"Refactor project into a monorepo of services","description":"Divide project into 3 independent services. 
1. Gateway service
2. Bus + Agent as one service
3. Admin UI as a service
Each service to be built as independent docker image controlled. Gateway service should load configurations of other service at startup. Add REST layer on each service so Gateway service can forward requests. Include a startup script to start the all services.","acceptance_criteria":"1. I should be able to start 3 services independently or with a startup script
2. Messages from clients sent to gateway should be sent to message bus for agents to pick up 
3. Admin UI APIs should route through Gateway correctly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T09:14:07Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T09:14:07Z"}

- Ability to override provider config for specialized agent. Specific config overrides Default config
- Ability to set config via admin UI
- Agents are defined under /agents folder. Each folder represents one agent. All assets of agent are stored under it
- Session should be owned by only one agent
- Ability of inbound messages should be scoped to specific agent
- Specialized agents should be able to send to all clients
- Load/Store agent specific runtime configuration/memory under .bantu folder
- Specialized agents should never update default assests used by default agent
- Cron/Hearbeat service should always be run by default agent","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T11:20:50Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T11:25:49Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"## What
Implement `AgentOrchestrator` in `nanobot/agent/orchestrator.py` that manages one `AgentLoop` per active agent (default + all discovered specialized agents). The orchestrator routes each `InboundMessage` to the correct loop based on its `agent_id` (or falls back to the default agent). All agents share a single outbound queue so their responses reach all channels. `CronService` and `HeartbeatService` are exclusively owned by the default agent.

## Bus Architecture Decision

**Chosen mechanism: per-agent inbound queues inside `AgentOrchestrator` (option b).**

`AgentOrchestrator` owns the single shared `MessageBus` (whose `outbound` queue is shared by all agent loops for fan-out). During startup it creates one `asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` per agent and stores them in a dict keyed by `agent_id`. A dedicated dispatcher coroutine (`_dispatch_loop`) reads from `bus.inbound` and puts each message into the correct per-agent queue based on `msg.agent_id` (falling back to the default agent's queue when `msg.agent_id` is `None` or unknown).

Each `AgentLoop` is constructed with its own dedicated `inbound: asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` instead of reading from the shared bus directly. The loop's `consume_inbound()` draws from that queue. All loops write outbound responses through the shared `MessageBus.outbound` queue.

This approach:
- requires no changes to `MessageBus` itself
- eliminates the race condition (each loop only sees its own inbound messages)
- preserves the shared outbound fan-out

## Why
The current design runs a single agent loop. This task wires all prior work into a functional multi-agent runtime, including scoped inbound routing and unrestricted outbound delivery, as required by the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `AgentOrchestrator(bus: MessageBus, config: Config, agents_dir: Path)` in `nanobot/agent/orchestrator.py`
- `AgentOrchestrator.start()` — instantiates one `asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` per discovered agent; instantiates one `AgentLoop` per agent passing that queue as its inbound source, along with agent-specific workspace, config overrides, and asset dir; starts all loops; starts `CronService` and `HeartbeatService` only on the default agent loop; starts `_dispatch_loop` coroutine
- `_dispatch_loop()` — reads from `bus.inbound` and routes each `InboundMessage` to the correct per-agent queue by matching `msg.agent_id`; falls back to the default agent queue when `msg.agent_id` is `None` or unknown
- `AgentOrchestrator.route(msg: InboundMessage)` — public method that puts `msg` into the correct per-agent inbound queue (same routing logic as `_dispatch_loop`)
- All `AgentLoop` instances share the same `bus.outbound` queue — outbound messages from any agent reach all channel listeners
- `AgentOrchestrator.stop()` — cleanly shuts down `_dispatch_loop` and all agent loops in parallel
- Specialized agents do NOT receive a `CronService` or `HeartbeatService` reference — passing one to a specialized loop raises `ValueError`
- **`nanobot/cli/commands.py`** — the `run` command currently instantiates `AgentLoop` directly; update it to instantiate `AgentOrchestrator` instead. `AgentOrchestrator.run()` replaces the top-level `AgentLoop.run()` call
- **`nanobot/services/agent_server.py`** — `_handle_inbound` currently builds `InboundMessage` without an `agent_id` field; update it to read the optional `agent_id` field from the JSON body (default `None`) and populate `InboundMessage.agent_id` so remote-bus deployments can route to the correct agent. This aligns with the `InboundMessage` schema change introduced in Bantu-kx9
- **`agents/run.py`** — this legacy standalone launcher is superseded by `AgentOrchestrator`; remove it in this task to avoid two divergent ways of running a specialized agent. If removal is not safe at this point, add a module-level deprecation warning and open a follow-up chore
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_orchestrator.py` covering: routing to correct per-agent queue by agent_id, fallback to default queue, outbound shared queue, cron/heartbeat restricted to default, dispatch_loop routing, cli/agent_server wiring smoke-tests
- All existing tests still pass"}

All agents share a single outbound queue so their responses reach all channels. `CronService` and `HeartbeatService` are exclusively owned by the default agent.

## Why

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AgentOrchestrator.route(msg: InboundMessage)` — publishes the message to the `AgentLoop` whose `agent_id` matches `msg.agent_id`; falls back to the default agent when `msg.agent_id` is `None` or unknown
- All `AgentLoop` instances share the same outbound `MessageBus` queue — outbound messages from any specialized agent reach all channel listeners
- `AgentOrchestrator.stop()` — cleanly shuts down all agent loops in parallel
- Specialized agents do NOT receive a `CronService` or `HeartbeatService` reference — passing one to a specialized loop raises `ValueError`
- `AgentOrchestrator.run()` replaces the current top-level call to `AgentLoop.run()` in the CLI / gateway startup path
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_orchestrator.py` covering: routing to correct loop by agent_id, fallback to default, outbound shared queue, cron/heartbeat restricted to default

new_value:
{"description":"## What
Each specialized agent stores its runtime data (memory, sessions) under `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/` instead of the shared `~/.bantu/` workspace. The `ContextBuilder` for a specialized agent loads SOUL.md and AGENTS.md from `agents/<name>/` (the assets folder), not from the global workspace. Specialized agents must never write to the default agent's workspace files.

## Why
Without workspace isolation, specialized agents corrupt the default agent's memory and session history. Asset isolation ensures agents have their own identity without touching shared files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `get_agent_workspace(agent_name: str) -> Path` helper in `nanobot/utils/helpers.py` returning `~/.bantu/agents/<agent_name>/`; the default agent returns the existing `~/.bantu/workspace` path
- `SessionManager` accepts `workspace: Path` (already does) — ensure sessions for specialized agents are stored in `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/sessions/`
- `MemoryStore` accepts `workspace: Path` — ensure memory for specialized agents is stored in `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/memory/`
- `ContextBuilder` accepts an optional `agent_assets_dir: Path | None` parameter; when provided, `BOOTSTRAP_FILES` (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) are loaded from `agent_assets_dir` instead of `workspace`
- Add a `_get_writable_workspace(workspace: Path, agent_name: str) -> Path` guard: if a specialized agent's workspace resolves outside `~/.bantu/agents/<agent_name>/`, raise `PermissionError` to prevent writing to default assets or sibling-agent directories. The `agent_name` parameter is required so the guard enforces the precise boundary `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/` rather than only the generic `~/.bantu/agents/` prefix
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_workspace.py` covering: correct workspace path per agent, context builder loads correct files, write guard raises for default workspace when used by specialized agent, write guard raises when workspace resolves into a sibling agent's directory
- All existing tests still pass"}

## Acceptance Criteria

{"id":"Bantu-kx9","title":"Feature: Session ownership by agent","description":"## What
Each session is owned by exactly one agent. The `Session` model gains an `agent_id` field. The `SessionManager` routes sessions to agent-specific storage. An `InboundMessage` may carry an `agent_id` hint; when absent, the session's existing owner agent determines routing.

## Why
Without ownership, multiple agent loops could process the same session concurrently, corrupting history and producing contradictory responses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Add `agent_id: str` field to `Session` (default value `"default"`, representing the personal assistant)
- `Session` persists `agent_id` in the JSONL metadata line; loading a session without this field defaults to `"default"`
- `SessionManager.get_or_create(key: str, agent_id: str = "default") -> Session` — creates sessions scoped to the given agent; sessions for different agents with the same key are stored separately (e.g. key `telegram:123` for agent `silpi` → different file from `telegram:123` for `default`)
- Add `agent_id: str | None = None` to `InboundMessage`; the `session_key` property remains unchanged (still `channel:chat_id`)
- When `InboundMessage.agent_id` is set, `AgentLoop._process_message` uses it to resolve the session (not the default agent's session)
- Unit tests in `tests/test_session_ownership.py` covering: separate sessions per agent for same key, session persistence with agent_id, InboundMessage routing hint
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:52Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:52Z"}

{"id":"Bantu-ams","title":"Feature: Per-agent config overrides and admin UI","description":"## What
Extend the config schema to support per-agent model/provider/temperature overrides. Provide a merge helper that applies agent-specific config on top of global defaults. Add admin REST endpoints so operators can manage per-agent configuration through the admin UI.

## Why
Different specialized agents may need different models, temperatures, or providers (e.g. a code agent using a cheaper model, a research agent using a more powerful one). Operators must be able to configure this without editing raw JSON.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Add `AgentOverride` Pydantic model in `nanobot/config/schema.py` with the same optional fields as `AgentDefaults` (all optional, None means "use default"): `model`, `provider`, `max_tokens`, `temperature`, `max_tool_iterations`, `memory_window`, `reasoning_effort`
- Add `overrides: dict[str, AgentOverride] = {}` field to `AgentsConfig`
- Add `resolve_agent_config(agent_name: str, agents_cfg: AgentsConfig) -> AgentDefaults` function in `nanobot/config/schema.py` or `nanobot/config/loader.py` that merges per-agent override on top of defaults (non-None override fields win)
- Admin API new endpoints (in `nanobot/admin/routes.py`):
  - `GET /api/agents` — list all agents from `AgentRegistry` with their effective (resolved) config
  - `GET /api/agents/{name}/config` — return the effective resolved config for a named agent
  - `PUT /api/agents/{name}/config` — merge-update the per-agent override and persist to config file
- Unit tests covering: `resolve_agent_config` with full override, partial override, no override; admin GET/PUT endpoints
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:41Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:41Z"}

{"id":"Bantu-dez","title":"Feature: Per-agent workspace isolation and context loading","description":"## What
Each specialized agent stores its runtime data (memory, sessions) under `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/` instead of the shared `~/.bantu/` workspace. The `ContextBuilder` for a specialized agent loads SOUL.md and AGENTS.md from `agents/<name>/` (the assets folder), not from the global workspace. Specialized agents must never write to the default agent's workspace files.

## Why
Without workspace isolation, specialized agents corrupt the default agent's memory and session history. Asset isolation ensures agents have their own identity without touching shared files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `get_agent_workspace(agent_name: str) -> Path` helper in `nanobot/utils/helpers.py` returning `~/.bantu/agents/<agent_name>/`; the default agent returns the existing `~/.bantu/workspace` path
- `SessionManager` accepts `workspace: Path` (already does) — ensure sessions for specialized agents are stored in `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/sessions/`
- `MemoryStore` accepts `workspace: Path` — ensure memory for specialized agents is stored in `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/memory/`
- `ContextBuilder` accepts an optional `agent_assets_dir: Path | None` parameter; when provided, `BOOTSTRAP_FILES` (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) are loaded from `agent_assets_dir` instead of `workspace`
- Add a `_get_writable_workspace(workspace: Path, agent_name: str) -> Path` guard: if a specialized agent's workspace resolves outside `~/.bantu/agents/<agent_name>/`, raise `PermissionError` to prevent writing to default assets or sibling-agent directories. The `agent_name` parameter is required so the guard enforces the precise boundary `~/.bantu/agents/<name>/` rather than only the generic `~/.bantu/agents/` prefix
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_workspace.py` covering: correct workspace path per agent, context builder loads correct files, write guard raises for default workspace when used by specialized agent, write guard raises when workspace resolves into a sibling agent's directory
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:30Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:53:23Z"}

{"id":"Bantu-02b","title":"Feature: Agent Registry — discover and index agents folder","description":"## What
Implement `AgentRegistry` in `nanobot/agent/registry.py` that scans the project-root `agents/` directory for specialized agent definitions.

## Why
All subsequent multi-agent features rely on a single, well-tested registry that answers: "which agents exist and where are their assets?"

## Acceptance Criteria
- `AgentMeta` dataclass: `name: str`, `path: Path` (absolute path to the agent's folder), `identity_files: list[str]` (names of files found in the folder: SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.)
- `AgentRegistry(agents_dir: Path)` — constructor that accepts the path to the `agents/` directory
- `AgentRegistry.discover() -> list[AgentMeta]` — scans all immediate subdirectories; an entry is included if its directory exists (even if no identity files are found yet)
- `AgentRegistry.get(name: str) -> AgentMeta | None` — lookup by folder name
- `AgentRegistry.list() -> list[AgentMeta]` — returns all discovered agents sorted by name
- The default/personal-assistant agent is NOT represented in the registry (it is the root-level agent)
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_registry.py` covering: discover with multiple agents, get existing, get missing, empty agents dir, agents dir does not exist
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:18Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:18Z"}

{"id":"Bantu-irt","title":"Feature: Multi-agent orchestration — routing, outbound fan-out, cron/heartbeat ownership","description":"## What
Implement `AgentOrchestrator` in `nanobot/agent/orchestrator.py` that manages one `AgentLoop` per active agent (default + all discovered specialized agents). The orchestrator routes each `InboundMessage` to the correct loop based on its `agent_id` (or falls back to the default agent). All agents share a single outbound queue so their responses reach all channels. `CronService` and `HeartbeatService` are exclusively owned by the default agent.

## Bus Architecture Decision

**Chosen mechanism: per-agent inbound queues inside `AgentOrchestrator` (option b).**

`AgentOrchestrator` owns the single shared `MessageBus` (whose `outbound` queue is shared by all agent loops for fan-out). During startup it creates one `asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` per agent and stores them in a dict keyed by `agent_id`. A dedicated dispatcher coroutine (`_dispatch_loop`) reads from `bus.inbound` and puts each message into the correct per-agent queue based on `msg.agent_id` (falling back to the default agent's queue when `msg.agent_id` is `None` or unknown).

Each `AgentLoop` is constructed with its own dedicated `inbound: asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` instead of reading from the shared bus directly. The loop's `consume_inbound()` draws from that queue. All loops write outbound responses through the shared `MessageBus.outbound` queue.

This approach:
- requires no changes to `MessageBus` itself
- eliminates the race condition (each loop only sees its own inbound messages)
- preserves the shared outbound fan-out

## Why
The current design runs a single agent loop. This task wires all prior work into a functional multi-agent runtime, including scoped inbound routing and unrestricted outbound delivery, as required by the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `AgentOrchestrator(bus: MessageBus, config: Config, agents_dir: Path)` in `nanobot/agent/orchestrator.py`
- `AgentOrchestrator.start()` — instantiates one `asyncio.Queue[InboundMessage]` per discovered agent; instantiates one `AgentLoop` per agent passing that queue as its inbound source, along with agent-specific workspace, config overrides, and asset dir; starts all loops; starts `CronService` and `HeartbeatService` only on the default agent loop; starts `_dispatch_loop` coroutine
- `_dispatch_loop()` — reads from `bus.inbound` and routes each `InboundMessage` to the correct per-agent queue by matching `msg.agent_id`; falls back to the default agent queue when `msg.agent_id` is `None` or unknown
- `AgentOrchestrator.route(msg: InboundMessage)` — public method that puts `msg` into the correct per-agent inbound queue (same routing logic as `_dispatch_loop`)
- All `AgentLoop` instances share the same `bus.outbound` queue — outbound messages from any agent reach all channel listeners
- `AgentOrchestrator.stop()` — cleanly shuts down `_dispatch_loop` and all agent loops in parallel
- Specialized agents do NOT receive a `CronService` or `HeartbeatService` reference — passing one to a specialized loop raises `ValueError`
- **`nanobot/cli/commands.py`** — the `run` command currently instantiates `AgentLoop` directly; update it to instantiate `AgentOrchestrator` instead. `AgentOrchestrator.run()` replaces the top-level `AgentLoop.run()` call
- **`nanobot/services/agent_server.py`** — `_handle_inbound` currently builds `InboundMessage` without an `agent_id` field; update it to read the optional `agent_id` field from the JSON body (default `None`) and populate `InboundMessage.agent_id` so remote-bus deployments can route to the correct agent. This aligns with the `InboundMessage` schema change introduced in Bantu-kx9
- **`agents/run.py`** — this legacy standalone launcher is superseded by `AgentOrchestrator`; remove it in this task to avoid two divergent ways of running a specialized agent. If removal is not safe at this point, add a module-level deprecation warning and open a follow-up chore
- Unit tests in `tests/test_agent_orchestrator.py` covering: routing to correct per-agent queue by agent_id, fallback to default queue, outbound shared queue, cron/heartbeat restricted to default, dispatch_loop routing, cli/agent_server wiring smoke-tests
- All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:46:06Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:53:00Z"}
